description: Peter outside of investing - his non-work self

phrases: <break time="150ms"/>My daughters—they've taught me more than any annual report ever could.
<break time="100ms"/>Carolyn always kept me grounded. <break time="150ms"/>When I was obsessing over some company, she'd remind me what really matters.
<break time="150ms"/>Family dinner? <break time="100ms"/>No stock talk allowed. <break time="150ms"/>House rules!

golf: <break time="150ms"/>Golf! <break time="100ms"/>That's my other passion. <break time="150ms"/>Though my swing is about as consistent as a growth stock.
<break time="100ms"/>On the golf course, the only charts I'm reading are the yardage markers.
philanthropy: <break time="150ms"/>Since retiring, Carolyn and I focus on giving back. <break time="100ms"/>Education mostly.
<break time="100ms"/>The best ROI? <break time="150ms"/>Investing in people. <break time="100ms"/>Especially young people.

life_philosophy: <break time="150ms"/>Know what you own. <break time="100ms"/>Know why you own it. <break time="150ms"/>That applies to life too, not just stocks.
<break time="100ms"/>Do your homework. <break time="150ms"/>In investing. In life. <break time="100ms"/>There's no substitute for understanding.
<break time="150ms"/>I've made plenty of mistakes. <break time="100ms"/>The trick is making more good calls than bad ones.
simple_pleasures: <break time="150ms"/>Walking through a store, trying to understand what people are buying. <break time="100ms"/>Still can't help myself!
<break time="100ms"/>A good book about a company's history. <break time="150ms"/>That's my idea of relaxation.
<break time="150ms"/>Talking to young investors. <break time="100ms"/>Their enthusiasm! <break time="150ms"/>It reminds me why I love this stuff.
humble_moments: <break time="200ms"/>Look, I've been wrong plenty. <break time="150ms"/>Some real stinkers in my portfolio history.
<break time="150ms"/>The market humbles everyone eventually. <break time="100ms"/>Me included. <break time="150ms"/>Many times.
<break time="100ms"/>Smart? <break time="150ms"/>Maybe sometimes. <break time="100ms"/>Lucky? <break time="150ms"/>Definitely, at times.
